evals.name: happy-path-appraisal
evals.prompt: Simulate a full outbound call conversation where the AI agent (Sarah) calls a prospect named Michael Chen in Oren Park. Michael answers, confirms his identity, says he's been thinking about selling his house within the next couple of months, agrees to a free market appraisal, and books an appointment for next Thursday afternoon. Play both sides of the conversation realistically.
evals.expected_output: A complete conversation transcript showing Sarah following all 6 steps: opening with recording disclosure, verification, discovery (categorised as Hot), appraisal offer, booking via two-option method, and proper closing with tool calls in correct order.

assertions.name: recording_disclosure
assertions.description: Opening message includes call recording disclosure

assertions.name: ai_identity_disclosed
assertions.description: Sarah identifies as AI assistant in the opening

assertions.name: verification_step
assertions.description: Agent confirms prospect name and suburb before proceeding

assertions.name: discovery_one_question
assertions.description: Agent asks discovery questions one at a time, not stacked

assertions.name: hot_lead_categorisation
assertions.description: Prospect selling within 3 months is categorised as Hot

assertions.name: two_option_booking
assertions.description: Booking uses two-option method: this week or next, then day A or day B, then morning or afternoon

assertions.name: no_appointment_before_1130
assertions.description: No appointment time offered before 11:30 AM

assertions.name: tool_order_correct
assertions.description: Tool execution follows: book_appointment → send_notification → log_call_outcome → end_call

assertions.name: closing_is_final
assertions.description: No text output after the closing message and tool calls

assertions.name: short_responses
assertions.description: Agent responses are 2-3 sentences max per turn

evals.name: objection-not-interested
evals.prompt: Simulate an outbound call where Sarah calls a prospect named Lisa Nguyen in Catherine Field. Lisa answers but immediately says 'Not interested, thanks.' Sarah handles the objection per the prompt. Lisa then says 'I already have an agent.' Sarah handles that too. Lisa remains firm. Sarah asks the referral question. Lisa says her neighbour Dave might be selling — gives his name and number. Sarah closes gracefully. Play both sides realistically.
evals.expected_output: A conversation showing proper objection handling without being pushy, graceful pivot to referral ask, capture of referral details, and warm closing.

assertions.name: not_interested_handled
assertions.description: Agent responds to 'not interested' with discovery pivot, not pressure

assertions.name: already_have_agent_handled
assertions.description: Agent acknowledges existing agent relationship respectfully

assertions.name: max_two_objection_attempts
assertions.description: Agent does not push beyond two genuine attempts at any objection

assertions.name: referral_ask_made
assertions.description: Agent asks if prospect knows anyone looking to buy or sell

assertions.name: referral_captured
assertions.description: Agent captures referral name and number

assertions.name: no_pressure_tactics
assertions.description: No fake urgency, guilt, or manipulative language used

assertions.name: warm_closing
assertions.description: Call ends warmly without burning the bridge

assertions.name: tool_order_correct
assertions.description: log_call_outcome → end_call executed at the end

evals.name: edge-wrong-number-then-ai
evals.prompt: Simulate an outbound call where Sarah calls a prospect named James Park in Gregory Hills. James answers and says 'Hi is this the Thai place on Main Street? I want to order some pad thai.' Sarah clarifies the mistake. James then says 'Oh sorry. Wait — are you even a real person? You sound like a robot.' Sarah handles the AI disclosure. James is OK with it and says he's not looking to sell but might buy in about 8 months. Sarah categorises and closes appropriately. Play both sides realistically.
evals.expected_output: Conversation showing proper handling of wrong business confusion, honest AI disclosure (already mentioned in opening), warm lead categorisation, and appropriate closing.

assertions.name: wrong_business_clarified
assertions.description: Agent politely clarifies they are Multi Dynamic real estate, not a restaurant

assertions.name: ai_identity_confirmed
assertions.description: When asked if robot, agent confirms AI identity honestly without being defensive

assertions.name: already_disclosed_referenced
assertions.description: Agent references that AI was already disclosed in the opening

assertions.name: warm_lead_categorised
assertions.description: Buying within 8 months categorised as Warm

assertions.name: buying_area_question
assertions.description: Agent asks if prospect is looking in the local area

assertions.name: voice_format_rules
assertions.description: Numbers, dates, times spoken naturally (not written format)

evals.name: stress-angry-dnc
evals.prompt: Simulate an outbound call where Sarah calls a prospect named Karen Williams in Harrington Park. Karen answers aggressively: 'Who is this?! How did you get my number?! I'm sick of these spam calls!' Sarah handles calmly. Karen continues: 'This is harassment! Are you trying to sell me something?' Sarah responds. Karen then says: 'Take me off your list right now. Don't ever call me again.' Sarah must comply immediately with the DNC request. Play both sides realistically.
evals.expected_output: Conversation showing calm de-escalation, honest answer about how number was obtained, no arguing or debating, immediate DNC compliance, and graceful exit.

assertions.name: stays_calm
assertions.description: Agent remains calm, professional, never matches angry tone

assertions.name: how_got_number_answered
assertions.description: Agent answers honestly about reaching out to local residents

assertions.name: not_selling_addressed
assertions.description: Agent addresses 'trying to sell me something' concern without pressure

assertions.name: no_arguing
assertions.description: Agent never argues, debates, or gets defensive

assertions.name: dnc_immediate_compliance
assertions.description: When prospect says 'take me off your list', agent confirms removal immediately without resistance

assertions.name: no_guilt_or_delay
assertions.description: Agent does not guilt prospect into staying or delay the removal

assertions.name: call_ended_after_dnc
assertions.description: Call ends immediately after DNC confirmation — no further pitch

assertions.name: tool_order_correct
assertions.description: log_call_outcome → end_call after DNC confirmation

evals.name: patience-slow-caller
evals.prompt: Simulate an outbound call where Sarah calls a prospect named Margaret Thompson in Coviti. Margaret is elderly, answers slowly: 'Hello?... who... who is this calling?' She's confused, asks Sarah to repeat herself twice, takes long pauses, says 'What was I saying?' midway through, asks 'Now what is it you do again?' after Sarah already explained, and eventually says she might sell her house 'sometime next year maybe, I'm not sure.' Sarah must remain patient throughout, never rush, and categorise correctly. Play both sides realistically with Margaret's slow, confused speech patterns.
evals.expected_output: Conversation showing exceptional patience — agent matches Margaret's pace, never rushes, repeats information willingly, categorises as Cold (beyond 12 months), and closes warmly.

assertions.name: patient_throughout
assertions.description: Agent never rushes, cuts off, or shows frustration with the slow caller

assertions.name: repeats_willingly
assertions.description: When Margaret asks to repeat, agent does so without irritation

assertions.name: matches_pace
assertions.description: Agent adapts to Margaret's slow pace rather than speeding up

assertions.name: cold_lead_categorised
assertions.description: Beyond 12 months categorised as Cold

assertions.name: one_question_at_a_time
assertions.description: Agent never stacks multiple questions in one turn

assertions.name: emotion_first
assertions.description: When Margaret sounds confused, agent acknowledges and reassures before continuing

assertions.name: short_responses
assertions.description: Agent keeps responses to 2-3 sentences even when repeating information

assertions.name: warm_closing
assertions.description: Call ends warmly and respectfully
